  • Home
  • Home

GreenhousesJL

Fast greenhouse climate simulation in Julia.

GreenhousesJL is a high-performance library for simulating the thermal behaviour of greenhouses. It solves the coupled energy balance equations of the cover, indoor air, floor, soil layers, walls, and (optionally) arbitrary interior objects, using the adaptive ODE solvers from the Julia SciML ecosystem.

Why GreenhousesJL?

FeatureGreenhousesJLOpenModelicaEnergyPlus
7-day simulation10–35 ms250 ms340–410 ms
SolverAdaptive (Tsit5)Adaptive (DASSL)Fixed 1-min step
LanguageJuliaModelica → CFortran/C++
Soil depth10 m (8 layers, T̄ annual)1.3 m (3 °C constant)Foundation:Kiva
Solar transmittanceFresnel angular + PAR/NIRConstant τ per bandPolynomial fit
Interior LW exchangeN-surface radiosityFloor↔cover onlyScriptF (15 surf)
View factorsMC geometry (3D vertices)Hottel rectanglesVertex-based
Beam solar distributionGeometric projection100% → floorFullExterior
Exterior convectionBot (roof) + Walton (walls)Bot forced onlyISO 15099 (glass)
Interior convectionBalemans (1989)BalemansTARP / ISO 15099
Heating systemsBoiler, HP, CHP + pipe networkCHP, HP, pipesManual EMS
PV panelsOpaque / semi / transparent—Manual EMS
Crop productivityTemperature + light response——
Geometry engineExtensible (add any surface)Fixed 3 nodesFixed IDF geometry
EP cross-validation4 climates × 6 months3 winter casesreference

What changed from the Modelica library

The original Greenhouses Modelica library (Altes-Buch 2019) provided cover, air, and floor energy balances. GreenhousesJL extends it significantly:

  • 4-wall model with per-cardinal N/S/E/W nodes and insulate() API
  • 10 m deep soil (8 exponentially increasing layers, annual-mean T_deep)
  • Fresnel angular transmittance (n=1.526, direct/diffuse split)
  • N-surface radiosity (analytically validated: cube 0.007% error)
  • MC geometry engine (3D vertex-based view factors, extensible to any object)
  • Geometric beam solar distribution (sun-angle dependent floor/wall split)
  • View factor bug fix (Hottel vf_perp_rect A/B swap: returned F₂₁ not F₁₂)

See the Validation page for the full cross-validation story.

EnergyPlus cross-validation

Systematic comparison against EnergyPlus 25.1 on the Savona 5×5 m bare greenhouse (walls = 60% of envelope) across 4 climates and 6 months revealed:

  • Winter agreement: +0.5 °C bias (Lyon December)
  • Summer gap: +4.3 °C (Lyon July) — traced to EP's ISO 15099 exterior convection on glass fenestration (h ≈ 50 W/(m²K)), which cannot be overridden via EMS. Julia uses Bot 1983 (h ≈ 7 W/(m²K)).
  • Martin & Monfet (2024) independently confirmed EP's interior convection is "ill-suited" for CEA/greenhouse spaces.

Full benchmark analysis: 25-slide presentation.

Physics at a glance

         ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  Sky ◄──┤ Q_rad (σεT⁴)                               │
         │                    ┌───────┐                │
  Out ◄──┤ Q_cnv (Bot/Walton) │ Cover │ ◄── Q_cnv(Bal) ◄── Air ──► Walls ──► Out
         │                    └───────┘                │          ──► Sky
         │                         ▲ Radiosity (N-surf)│
         │                    ┌────┴──┐                │
         │                    │ Floor │ ◄── Q_cnv ◄── Air
         │                    └───┬───┘                │
         │                   Soil layers (8, exp)      │
         │                   10 m depth                │
         │                   T_deep = T̄(EPW)           │
         └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Each node has a thermal capacity (mass × specific heat) and exchanges heat with neighbours through convection, long-wave radiation (radiosity enclosure), solar absorption (Fresnel angular + PAR/NIR multi-bounce), and conduction.

Quick start

using GreenhousesJL

# Load weather data (EPW format)
epw = load_epw("weather/lyon.epw")
tmy = epw_to_tmy(epw; start_month=12, n_days=7)

# Define a bare greenhouse (14 000 m², no heating)
p = GreenhouseParams(tmy; A=14000.0)
u0 = initial_state(p; T0=278.15)

# Solve for 7 days
sol = solve(ODEProblem(greenhouse_rhs!, u0, (0.0, 604800.0), p), Tsit5();
            reltol=1e-6, saveat=3600.0)

# T_deep is auto-computed from annual mean T_out
# Soil depth: 10.2 m (8 exponential layers)
# Solar: Fresnel angular correction applied automatically

Adding walls and insulation

# Savona 5×5 m with walls
p = SavonaParams(tmy; with_walls=true)

# Insulate the north wall
p_ins = SavonaParams(tmy; with_walls=true,
                     walls=insulate(GlassWalls(L=5,W=5,h_gutter=2,rise=2.165),
                                   :north; U=0.4))

Adding interior objects (geometry engine)

# Define surfaces by 3D vertices
surfaces = savona_surfaces()

# Add a heating pipe
push!(surfaces, make_surface("pipe",
    [SVector(1,1,0.5), SVector(4,1,0.5),
     SVector(4,1.1,0.5), SVector(1,1.1,0.5)];
    ε=0.9, α_solar=0.3, capacity=4186*0.5))

# Build enclosure with MC view factors
enc = build_enclosure(surfaces; N_mc=50000)

Designed for coupling

GreenhousesJL is designed to integrate with:

  • VirtualPlantLab.jl, 3D functional-structural plant modelling with Monte Carlo ray-tracing for PAR;
  • PlantBiophysics.jl, leaf-level photosynthesis (FvCB), stomatal conductance (Medlyn) and transpiration (Stanghellini).

See the Integration page for the coupling architecture.

Citing

If you use GreenhousesJL in your research, please cite:

Duchaufour, M. (2026). GreenhousesJL: Fast greenhouse climate simulation in Julia. ERMESS GREEN project, Université Savoie Mont Blanc / LOCIE.

And the original Greenhouses Modelica library:

Altes-Buch, Q., Quoilin, S. & Lemort, V. (2019). Greenhouses: A Modelica Library for the Simulation of Greenhouse Climate and Energy Systems. Proceedings of the 13th International Modelica Conference, Regensburg.
